lastOriginRCodes.title: Last Origin R+ Codes
lastOriginRCodes.intro: Last Origin R+ does not need fake code padding. This section tracks official coupon availability, VFUN reward channels, launch promotions, and expired-code status so players know where real rewards come from.

items.label: Active Public Coupons
items.reward: No public redeem code is shown on the official VFUN, X, or Facebook channels.
items.note: Use this card to prevent fake code claims while keeping the code module useful for searchers.

items.label: Launch Milestone Rewards
items.reward: Resource Pack, Genetic Material, Constantia S2 Battle Suit, Cyclops Princess, Bloodstained Eyepatch, Battle Log, Training Manual, Blind Princess, and Special Alternate Core.
items.note: These were announced as global pre-registration milestone rewards.

items.label: Official Reward Hub
items.reward: Game access, platform news, launcher information, and future official reward notices.
items.note: Players should verify coupons through VFUN or official social posts before using any reposted code.

items.label: Expired Coupons
items.reward: No expired official coupon list is published in the checked official channels.
items.note: Keep this card for future expired-code tracking after official coupon campaigns begin.

lastOriginRBeginnerGuide.title: Last Origin R+ Beginner Guide
lastOriginRBeginnerGuide.intro: Last Origin R+ is a strategy RPG about commanding biroids in turn-based grid battles. New players should focus on safe squad setup, early training, and steady story progression before chasing advanced builds.

steps.title: Start on your preferred platform
steps.details: Last Origin R+ is available through Android, iOS, and the VFUN platform. Mobile players can install from Google Play or the App Store, while PC players should use the official VFUN page.

steps.title: Understand the battle loop
steps.details: Each fight uses turn-based tactical combat. Your goal is to position units correctly, use each character’s strengths, and clear enemies while keeping the squad safe.

steps.title: Use the 9-grid formation system
steps.details: The official store description highlights a 9-grid battlefield. Place durable units where they can protect the team and keep fragile damage dealers away from direct pressure.

steps.title: Train characters before spreading resources
steps.details: Character growth and skill enhancement are core progression systems. Early resources should go into units that help clear campaign stages consistently.

steps.title: Push campaign and story stages
steps.details: Story stages introduce the ruined-world setting and unlock the main flow of progression. New accounts should clear stable stages first instead of rushing difficult content.

steps.title: Use launch rewards for account growth
steps.details: Pre-registration rewards included Resource Packs, Genetic Material, Battle Log, Training Manual, Blind Princess, Cyclops Princess, and Special Alternate Core. These rewards are useful for early roster setup and growth.

lastOriginRTierList.title: Last Origin R+ Tier List
lastOriginRTierList.intro: Because Last Origin R+ is built around 9-grid combat, early value comes from team function more than single-unit hype. This tier list ranks starter roles and confirmed launch reward targets by how useful they are for safe progression.

entries.name: Durable Frontline Defender
entries.reason: A safe frontline helps protect attackers and supports in grid battles, making early story clears more stable.

entries.name: Reliable Main Attacker
entries.reason: A consistent damage dealer speeds up campaign stages and makes resource farming easier.

entries.name: Blind Princess
entries.reason: Blind Princess was listed as a 50K pre-registration milestone reward together with Special Alternate Core, making her an important early account target.

entries.name: Turn-control or AP Support
entries.reason: Turn-based combat rewards units that help the squad act safely and keep the main attacker active.

entries.name: Cyclops Princess
entries.reason: Cyclops Princess was listed as a 30K pre-registration milestone reward, giving new players another valuable roster target.

entries.name: Training Manual and Battle Log Investment
entries.reason: These launch rewards support faster early character growth and should go into your most reliable starter units.

entries.name: Single-Target Boss Attacker
entries.reason: Useful when a stage needs focused damage, but less flexible than a general campaign carry.

entries.name: Niche Elemental Attacker
entries.reason: Element-focused units can be strong when the stage favors them, but they are less universal for first-week progression.

entries.name: Constantia S2 Battle Suit
entries.reason: This was listed as a 10K milestone reward and is useful as part of the launch reward package, but it is not the main reroll target.

entries.name: Unbuilt Filler Unit
entries.reason: A unit with no investment or no clear job should not take priority over your defender, main attacker, and support core.

entries.name: Skin-Only Target
entries.reason: R+ exclusive skins are valuable for collectors, but they do not replace early squad stability.

lastOriginRRerollGuide.title: Last Origin R+ Reroll Guide
lastOriginRRerollGuide.intro: Rerolling in Last Origin R+ should be practical, not endless. A good starter account should secure useful launch rewards, build a balanced 9-grid squad, and invest early resources into units that help clear story stages.

steps.title: Claim official launch and milestone rewards first
steps.details: Start by collecting available official rewards such as Resource Packs, Genetic Material, Battle Log, Training Manual, Blind Princess, Cyclops Princess, and Special Alternate Core.
steps.keepRule: Keep accounts that can immediately strengthen a main team.

steps.title: Prioritize team function over rare-name chasing
steps.details: Because combat uses grid positioning and turn-based tactics, a strong account needs a defender, a reliable attacker, and useful support more than one isolated unit.
steps.keepRule: Keep accounts with a clear frontline plus a safe damage core.

steps.title: Value Blind Princess and Cyclops Princess as launch targets
steps.details: Both names appeared in pre-registration milestone rewards, making them important early account targets for players starting during the global launch window.
steps.keepRule: Keep accounts that secure these launch reward targets and enough resources to build around them.

steps.title: Spend early growth resources carefully
steps.details: Training Manual, Battle Log, Genetic Material, and Resource Packs should support your best campaign-clearing units first.
steps.keepRule: Keep accounts where resources can build one stable team instead of spreading across many units.

steps.title: Do a light reroll only
steps.details: Rerolling is useful if the account misses key launch rewards or starts with a weak team shape. Once you have a stable defender-attacker-support core, progression is more valuable than restarting repeatedly.
steps.keepRule: Stop rerolling when your account can clear early story stages safely.

lastOriginRTeamBuilds.title: Last Origin R+ Team Builds
lastOriginRTeamBuilds.intro: Use this section to plan practical teams around role balance instead of raw power only. Start with a protected damage core, add a durable front line, then improve support and skill growth as stages become harder.

items.title: Balanced Starter Squad
items.description: Use one main damage dealer, one durable front unit, and support or utility units around them. This fits the game focus on commanding a squad, arranging units across a 9-grid battlefield, and maximizing each unit's strengths.
bullets: Put durable units in the cells most likely to take hits.
Keep the main damage dealer away from exposed positions.
Train the units you actually keep in the active lineup.

items.title: Protected Damage Core
items.description: Build around the unit that clears enemies most reliably, then use formation to keep that unit safe. Skill enhancement matters because character training and skill growth are core progression systems.
bullets: Upgrade the main carry before spreading resources too thin.
Place support near the damage core when the stage pattern allows it.
Change the grid setup when enemies threaten the back line.

items.title: Frontline Safety Build
items.description: Use durable units in exposed cells so fragile allies can keep acting. This is useful for players stuck in campaign or event stages where careful positioning matters more than simple auto-battle power.
bullets: Use the front row to absorb pressure.
Recheck formation after every new stage type.

items.title: Repeat Farming Team
items.description: For farming, prioritize consistency over risky burst damage. A safer team that clears the same stage repeatedly is more valuable than a stronger-looking team that fails when turn order or enemy targeting changes.
bullets: Use a lineup that survives bad targeting.
Keep skill upgrades focused on the repeat-clear team.
Replace weak links only after the current stage is stable.

lastOriginRCombatFormationGuide.title: Last Origin R+ Combat and Formation Guide
lastOriginRCombatFormationGuide.intro: This guide explains how to think during battle: read the grid, protect the carry, control exposed cells, and time skills around turn order. It is made for new players who want a clear combat plan before pushing harder campaign or event stages.

steps.title: Read the stage grid first
steps.description: Battles use dynamic grid stages, so the first job is to check where enemies can pressure your squad. Do not treat every stage as the same formation puzzle.
steps.tip: Move or rebuild the squad layout when enemy attacks reach your protected cells.

steps.title: Place durable units in danger cells
steps.description: The game emphasizes keeping allies safe. Put durable front units where attacks are most likely to land, then place fragile units behind or away from pressure.
steps.tip: A good formation prevents damage before healing or recovery is needed.

steps.title: Protect the main damage dealer
steps.description: A squad should maximize each unit's strengths across the 9-grid battlefield. Damage units usually give more value when they survive long enough to act every round.
steps.tip: If your best attacker dies early, the formation is wrong even if the unit is strong.

steps.title: Use skills with turn order in mind
steps.description: Last Origin R+ uses an individual turn system, so skill timing matters. Set up buffs, protection, or control before committing your main damage action.
steps.tip: Do not spend key skills before the unit that benefits from them can act.

steps.title: Upgrade formation and skills together
steps.description: Character training and skill enhancement are both part of progression. A strong unit still needs the right cell, and a good formation still needs upgraded skills to keep up with harder fights.
steps.tip: When a stage fails, check both placement and skill investment before replacing the whole team.

lastOriginRCharactersAndUnits.title: Last Origin R+ Characters and Units
lastOriginRCharactersAndUnits.intro: Last Origin R+ is character-driven, but the safest early approach is to compare units by role, formation fit, skill value, and current update relevance. This module highlights character data from official store pages and launch coverage.

rows.confirmedData: Last Origin R+ is a post-apocalyptic strategy RPG where players fight alongside biroids and command their own squad.
rows.playerUse: Use this as the base description for unit list, combatant guide, and character overview searches.

rows.confirmedData: The store pages describe training characters, enhancing skills, and assembling an ultimate lineup.
rows.playerUse: Prioritize units that fit your active squad and keep their skill upgrades focused.

rows.confirmedData: Units are arranged across a 9-grid battlefield to maximize each unit's strengths.
rows.playerUse: Compare units by where they can safely stand, what they protect, and how they support the main damage plan.

rows.confirmedData: Confirmed cast names include Yui Horie, Aya Suzaki, Emiri Kato, Yuu Serizawa, Osamu Hiyama, Reina Ueda, Kana Asumi, Manami Numakura, Yumi Hara, Lansbury Arthur, Aimi Tanaka, Chinatsu Akasaki, Mimu Kubota, Noriko Shitaya, Madoka Yonezawa, Himika Akaneya, and Sayuri Hara.
rows.playerUse: Use this row for voice actor, character profile, and presentation-focused searches.

rows.confirmedData: Launch coverage announced 10 exclusive R+ service skins, including a R+ remake of the New Teacher Leprechaun skin for T-3 Leprechaun.
rows.playerUse: Separate R+ exclusive cosmetics from normal character growth and standard Last Origin skin references.

rows.confirmedData: Recent store update notes list new combatants, new special skins, new event stories, permanent event stories, multi-language support, and new event items.
rows.playerUse: Keep character pages fresh by connecting new units and skins to the latest version update.

lastOriginREventsAndUpdateLog.title: Last Origin R+ Events and Update Log
lastOriginREventsAndUpdateLog.intro: Use this update log for returning players who want to know what changed recently. It focuses on official store notes, VALOFE/VFUN service links, official social posts, and launch coverage.

entries.details: Last Origin R+ launched globally as a version designed for global users.
entries.playerImpact: New players can start from the global service and follow launch rewards, account setup, and early progression events.

entries.update: Commemorative Promotions and Double EXP Event
entries.details: Launch coverage reports commemorative promotions, a double experience event, and progression packages prepared for early content.
entries.playerImpact: Good timing for new accounts to push early stages, train characters, and build a first stable squad.

entries.update: New Event Stories and Permanent Event Stories
entries.details: The latest store update notes list new event stories and permanent event stories added.
entries.playerImpact: Returning players should check story content, event access, and available rewards.

entries.update: New Combatants, Special Skins, and Event Items
entries.details: The latest store update notes list new combatants, new special skins, and new event items.
entries.playerImpact: Use this update for character pages, skin pages, event item guides, and farming priority content.

entries.details: Launch and store update coverage describe multi-language support for the global service.
entries.playerImpact: Helpful for players choosing the correct region, client, and language setting.

entries.details: Launch coverage announced 10 exclusive R+ service skins, including a R+ version of New Teacher Leprechaun for T-3 Leprechaun.
entries.playerImpact: Skin collectors should track R+ exclusive skins separately from standard Last Origin content.

faq.title: Last Origin R
faq.subtitle: Key platform, progression, and update answers for Last Origin R players

questions.question: Is Last Origin R singleplayer?
questions.answer: Last Origin R is built around single-account squad progression with tactical turn-based combat and story advancement.

questions.question: Where can I play Last Origin R?
questions.answer: Last Origin R is available on VFUN for PC and through Google Play and the App Store for mobile players.

questions.question: How should beginners start in Last Origin R?
questions.answer: New players should stabilize one defender-attacker-support core, use safe 9-grid placement, and focus upgrades on the active clear team.

questions.question: Does Last Origin R need rerolling?
questions.answer: Last Origin R rerolling should stay light: keep accounts that secure launch rewards and can clear early stages with a stable team core.

questions.question: How often does Last Origin R receive updates?
questions.answer: Last Origin R global updates include new combatants, skins, event stories, and event items, so checking the update log is useful for returning players.

questions.question: Where should I verify Last Origin R code announcements?
questions.answer: Use official Last Origin R channels such as VFUN Library and the official social accounts before trusting reposted code claims.
